summary: The conjecture that every simple graph with maximum degree $\Delta$ has a proper acyclic edge-colouring using at most $\Delta+2$ colours remains open. Since the problem was posted, the general upper bound has been reduced below $4\Delta-4$, reaching approximately $3.142(\Delta-1)+1$ as of 2026, and the conjecture has been confirmed for several special classes including 3-sparse graphs; moreover, Delcourt, Li, and Postle announced in 2023 that $(1+o(1))\Delta$ colours always suffice, though the exact Fiamčík bound is still unproved for general graphs.

since_posted.title: Acyclic Edge Coloring of 3-sparse Graphs
since_posted.authors: Nevil Anto, Manu Basavaraju, Shashanka Kulamarva
since_posted.claim: Proves Fiamčík's conjecture ($a'(G) \leq \Delta+2$) for all 3-sparse graphs, i.e., graphs in which every edge has at least one endpoint of degree at most 3.

since_posted.title: An improvement on the bound for the acyclic chromatic index
since_posted.authors: Lefteris Kirousis, John Livieratos, Alexandros Singh
since_posted.claim: Proves the general upper bound $a'(G) \leq 3.142(\Delta-1)+1$ for any graph with maximum degree $\Delta$, improving the previous best bound due to Fialho et al. (2020).

since_posted.title: Upper Bounds on the Acyclic Chromatic Index of Degenerate Graphs
since_posted.authors: Nevil Anto, Manu Basavaraju, Suresh Manjanath Hegde, Shashanka Kulamarva
since_posted.claim: Proves $a'(G) \leq \Delta+5$ for all 3-degenerate graphs, narrowing the gap to the conjectured $\Delta+2$ for this class.

since_posted.title: Planar graphs are acyclically edge $(\Delta + 5)$-colorable
since_posted.claim: Shows that every planar graph satisfies $a'(G) \leq \Delta+5$ via discharging, giving the current best bound for planar graphs while the conjecture ($\Delta+2$) remains open for this class.

notes: An important announced result — Delcourt, Li, and Postle, 'The Acyclic Edge Coloring Conjecture holds asymptotically,' showing $(1+o(1))\Delta$ colours suffice — was presented at the Georgia Tech Graph Theory Seminar on 17 October 2023, but no arXiv preprint or journal URL was locatable; it cannot be included in since_posted. A 2022 arXiv preprint (arXiv:2202.13846, Kirousis–Livieratos) claimed the stronger bound $2\Delta-1$ but was subsequently withdrawn due to errors in key lemmas and must not be cited. The intermediate general-bound improvement by Fialho et al. (2020) — referenced as the prior best in arXiv:2602.14859 — was not independently verified and is therefore not listed.
